Do not take this class or professor. It appears easy on the surface, but the midterm was awful. He does not give a study guide, and told students to review all class material to prepare. The class avg ended up being a 71 (in a comms class btw) and he was actually mad a students for being "underprepared." Professor is egotistical and unsympathetic.

Amazing teacher. Lectures clear and to the point, with examples. Prof Huppin is very impressive yet very accessible and kind. Will not baby you, but fair and if you follow directions and take the class seriously you will be fine. Learned a lot, which is the goal. Subject matter was fascinating. My favorite class I've taken. No unnecessary busywork.

I love, respect and admire this professor. Always available, clear instruction. She made Stat doable. Amazing! I personally recommend Dr. Hart as professor. Very encouraging and carrying professor. Thank you.
Wonderful human being, excellent professor and very helpful. I'll have her for all my classes any time. Respect!

Easy A! This class is online and lectures are pre-recorded. Her slides are very informative and pretty enjoyable! She gives excellent feedback and responds to all emails! All assignments are straightforward, weekly discussion posts, 5 quizzes, 4 assignments, a midterm, and one final with extra credit overall great professor!
Good class, really easy
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: She's assessable to reach through her email, she's clear on what you need to do by sending in announcements. We usually read off the textbook alot for the discussion posts, make sure to take notes on the slides she uploads and on the chapters you read because you'll need them on your exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Doesn't know what he's doing, I'd suggest you take someone else honestly.

He showed up late to class several times and sometimes seemed like he hadn't done the readings that he himself assigned to us. The lectures were ok. He is friendly but really hard to get ahold of if you email. Better to go through one of the TAs. It's an easy class though and if you just want the GE, go for it
More like a high school elective course than a college class. Super easy A even if you don't show up. You won't learn anything new if you do come to class. But Professor Herrera is a nice guy.

his class was interesting, but i think hes busy and its not his top priority, but that worked in my favor tbh it was super easy. had a group project/presentation that was pretty ok too. cancelled a couple classes, got dismissed early for a couple others.

Tyrone Howard is one of a kind. He is more compassionate than any professor at UCLA. He is there for all students that show an interest and will go beyond for those that have aspirations to pursue careers in education, as well as counseling. His lectures are amazing and he engages students with critical discussions on key topics. Take him--trust me

After taking Educ 130 with Professor Howard, I have never felt more determined to pursue a career path in education. He really cares about his student's educational goals and I learned a lot about my privileges and purposes from this class. I would take him for any other class that he offers. He's funny, approachable and someone to get to know!

He is the absolute BEST professor on this campus for ed and otherwise. Such a salient teaching style and so willing to help you in whatever way he can. (Bonus: Build a close working relationship with him and he will save your life time and time again.)
wonderful teacher, made me far more award of culture and race than ever before!

THE BEST professor EVER! I looked forward to every single class of his! 2 page journal every other week, one midterm 5 pgs final 15 page paper but it is sooooo worth it!! EVERY prof at UCLA should learn how to teach from him! you HAVE to take this class, its AWESOME!! HE IS THE BEST!
Take any class you can with him.  He is an outstanding individual and remarkable professor.  He made my UCLA experience very positive.

Extremely strict grader. All his feedback on the papers he will return to you will barely have anything positive in them. I thought I had a good understanding of the material we went over in class. He will absolutely nitpick your paper so make sure you repeatedly go over it and make it the best paper you have ever written.

I have read a couple of posts saying that Huehls is nice when you get to know him. This was not the experience I had. However, his classes are meaningfully challenging and he grades very fair. I would suggest his classes to anyone who has a thick skin and is up for a challenge.

Prof. Huehls is a brilliant professor because he is unapologetic in what he expects of his students. He holds us to high standards and doesn't shy away from complex ideas. But though the material is tough, he doesn't just leave us to figure it out - he is very thorough in his lectures. He's hilarious and both creatively/intellectually stimulating.

This class was by far the most difficult I have taken in my three years of college. I thought I was following along just fine, but my grades in the class reflected otherwise. Everyone in my department raves about him, but I didn't really care for the class, and I will not be taking classes with him in future.

By far the best professor I've ever had.  His classes are not easy by any means, but if you're willing to put in the work you will get back what you've given tenfold.  He's engaging, hilarious, and does the best job possible to make certain his students understand his lectures.

Maybe the best professor I've had. He's funny enough to keep you totally engaged through frequently difficult material, and teaches well enough to justify how tough he is. He loves to engage his students, but doesn't force participation. Wish I could take more classes with him.

This is one of the greatest classes that I have ever taken. He is very passionate about what he teaches. This is a great class and you will leave it wanting to have learned more. Great teacher, great texts, great class. Don't get me wrong, this is not an easy class, but you will love the challenge!

This professor is awful, she explains one thing in class then makes it 10x more difficult on the exams, her practice exams don't reflect the difficulty nor the material on her real exams, I would not recommend taking her for anything, the amount of work you have to put into this class is insane, its more than in pretty much any other class

I hated 100B, she designs the trickiest exams possible..its very hard to get a good score even if you know the material well. Also the grading in this class depends a great deal on your TA, if you get a good TA you will get a good grade, if you get a bad TA then you're screwed. I suggest you take another teacher for 100B, they are likely easier.

It is incredible how you can read the book, do the practice exams, and still do miserably poor on the actual exams because the exams are created to trick you. They want you to fail. Make sure you know how to decipher cryptic messages because there is nothing clear about this class. The professor needs a lesson in proper email etiquette; super rude.

She called me a lady, which is nice.  The class is way better than some other courses I have taken in the psychology dept.  She also wrote a book, which is also super.  The psych major is impacted, so it's rough out there, but I do recall this as a better class I had taken.

I took the 1st midterm yest. & let me tell you it was designed to trick u. She gives a disclaimer saying "students claim its harder than the practice exam and thats bc people take it w/ the key and they are in a different state-of-mind during the real exam," but NO. That was RIDICULOUS. I get its curved but whats the point if she TRIES to trick u??

She wasn't hard! just read summaries and know terms from book. know lecture and be able to apply. Exam answers are not tricky but you really need to understand the question. You do not need to make any assumptions, guesses. If you see the obvious answers or answer then pick it. the paragraph given or the question will include the answer of the ?s

i hope the next time she is late more than 10 minutes for ANYTHING that she stops to reflect on the ridiculous guidelines for the class. extremely confusing exams designed to trick you instead of testing knowledge or learning. most useless class about the most useless subject ever. i hope the TAs continue to do useless research while making 60k/yr
Entertaining teacher.
Wonderful, entertaining professor.
soo nice very clear, love her
No Comments
distant and rude
Nice people, perfect lecture
His exam and final sucks. Does not invloved on the homework he assigned. He doesn't know how to teach. I wondered why UCLA hasn't fired him yet. His class was like milk. It only good for 1 weeks
Hes the worst. DONT TAKE HIM

He was Absolutely HORRIBLE. you cant at all understand a word of what he speaks. and really vain. Very unhelpful. Infact the t.a can teach a million times better than he does. if u ask him quest. he gets all confused and makes u confused. this was the worst class i have ever taken.
Worst professor I've ever taken at UCLA! Avoid at all costs!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: This lady now teaches at Texas State university. She is by far the worst professor I have ever taken. DO NOT take this lady. The way she grades is insane! You must get every part of the work correct to get any credit. To make matters worse SHE CAN NOT SPEAK ENGLISH so understanding her lectures is near impossible you will be teaching yourself the material if you take this course.

Worst professor I have  taken. Her lectures are worthless dont bother taking notes she goes WAY to fast. Her English is terrible. Homework is difficult and practice quizzes, midterms, and final were also hard. She posted the answers but it didn't help because I couldn't find out how the professor solved the problems etc.
Absolutely terrible. Lectures were useless and she gave pop quizzes to screw people over. Tries to catch you at your worst by giving hard pop quizzes and difficult homework.
She is a really hard professor.  Do not miss lecture or the sections/labs.  You will learn more from your TA's IF they know what they're talking about.  I highly recommend a tutor.
this woman cannot speak English.

Loved him.  Makes his student think.  Is open to true intellectual thinking.  Opinions are not allowed.  This is GOOD.  To many of my fellow student thought opinions are driven by intelect, and not assumptions. Dr. Lovas, was a fantastic teach for Behavior Modification course!
Cranky, boring, narrow-minded, and he expects his substandard word usage to be parroted back on tests. A living demonstration of the limits of Behaviorism as a legitimate field of study.
he is MARVOLOUS !  ABA RULES!

Rating Professor Sempliner a 1 feels generous. His lectures were disorganized and unnecessarily complicated, and were completely different from his exams. I advise reading the textbook, as that offers a much clearer interpretation of the material. He would also often snap back at students who asked questions.
Run. Don't even think about it. Take the class another quarter.
Disorganized lecture and was absent for a few weeks, which was not supplemented by making the course any more lenient

Never recorded lectures & they were disorganized. Never posted any lecture notes, except once. Midterms and exams were not representative of the content we saw in class. He gave a practice midterm and provided no solutions. The midterms felt so long and complicated (5 questions with multiple parts).

Had him for 32A. Lectures were disorganized, at times he didn't know how to solve problems he was writing. Exams were not representative of the homework/textbook difficulty. Didn't provide extra material for practice, we had to ask repeatedly for it. He was insistent on no curve till he realized a good chunk of students would fail if he did so.

Professor Smurda makes Social Influence engaging and relevant by clearly explaining concepts and connecting them to real life. The lectures are organized, interesting, and reflect what actually shows up on exams, which makes the class feel fair and manageable. I left the course thinking differently about everyday behavior and social situations.

This was my favorite class at UCLA. She is a good communicator and her lectures were so interesting because she talks about current events that are happening right now and the research applies to everything in the world. Her tests are not easy, but you will be fine if you study.

Very interesting lectures. I can't believe someone is complaining about having to read an easy book for a college class. It's actually on the best seller list. This class is probably the only useful psychology class that I've ever taken. The professor loves her students and will spend a lot of time discussing the material with you.
This is by far my favorite class. The lectures were outstanding and Dr. Smurda really cares about her students.

Dr. Smurda's class is fun and interesting. It took me a while to actually understand everything, but once I did, it blew me away. She is also a great person to talk to and to get advice about graduate school. I enjoyed her class so much that I ended up taking 2 more with her.
Favorite class
I found this class to be very interesing and informative. Her tests were hard though so you need to study. I like the fact that there was a discussion component so we could talk about the material.

This was the worst class hands down. The readings were redundant. She assigned multiple readings on the same subject and none provide any new information, so clear your schedule because youll be reading A LOT. The lectures are dry and boring. And, the exams are written to confuse you. I dont understand why some professors and TAs do this.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: 135- Social Psych The class itself is cool/interactive. Two Midterms  40-50 MC questions There were 4 books assigned, however you're only responsible for a few chapters/book. Easy read & very interesting. 6 dis sections for this quarter, only accountable for 5. Must submit a post on readings.
I'm in love with her
She's amazing, hilarious, very intelligent. I highly recommend her
By far, the best professor on campus. She has changed my thinking about life more generally.
great teacher and very fair on exams. she is very easy to understand but i would recommend reading the textbook because it goes more in depth. favorite class though.

By far the best science teacher I have ever had. She is clear, helpful and entertaining as well. Her tests are extremely fair. There is a lot of information covered but that is the only reason I'd say easiness is not a 5. I would recommend attending her lectures because she goes into more detail than her slides, but her slides are posted.
Amazing teacher
Epic Professor! Can't go wrong! Highly Recommend!

JUKKA IS THE BEST! His practice exams were so helpful so TAKE THEM! He does give a lot of homework but he does office hours to go over questions on Sundays so if you are lost last minute these are a great resource. He is a great guy and even better professor. He offers so much extra help and he is reasonable in grading. I LOVE JUKKA!
this is a corona time review TAKE JUKKA. HES THE BEST PROFESSOR FOR LS30A. YOU DONT NEED TO WASTE MORE TIME FILTERING THROUGH REVIEWS FOR HIM JUST TAKE HIS CLASS. best, F+P
One of the best professors ucla has.

Hilarious man, funniest professor I've had thus far. Huge goofball. Loves his students and will listen to their feedback. He even lightened the homework load for us when we requested it. Will do whatever it takes to make sure you are prepared for exams. Supplies great resources, and keeps all lecture slides on CCLE.

Double PH.D eccentric guy who is willing to open up any of his specific view on comedy and his teaching is very theoretical. If you take his class be prepared to really study and review the topics. In the future you'll be really well off and you'll also do well on tests.

Jukka is an amazing professor, and his clarity and drive to make sure everyone understood the material was appreciated. Dr. Park, made learning physics as interactive and understandable as possible. He made me fall in love with physics again!!

Dr. Park relates classroom material to real life examples, which greatly helps to better understand concepts. Dr. Park's teaching method encourages students to go beyond memorizing, and pushes toward applying your understanding beyond the scope of the classroom.

The course was taught well, professor thoroughly explained the concepts. The lecture notes and check your understanding handouts were extremely helpful. The exit tasks and weekly homework

Dr Park is an excellent instructor. He cares about his students and makes sure we understand the content. His lectures are very interesting and thanks to the exercises we were able to commit the

it was an amazing course, i really enjoyed it. Best Physics class I've taken in my life. And I've taken about 6 course and I finally understand through his teaching methods.

Dr. Park says he will change the way we look at the world, and he was right. I learned so much!!! It's not just for my career but for my life. I wish I would have had Dr. Park and this class knowledge long ago in my life. Incredibly practical and applicable in so much of life. You really do understand so much more about how everything
Dr. Park is good at engaging the students in his lectures. Each lesson is fun and refreshing. He ismeticulous in his syllabus, so students know what to expect.

Dr. Park was a great instructor to have. He was very informative in every lecture and brought a sense ofhumour that put a smile on my face during every lesson. Overall I really enjoyed this class! Probably one of the lowest stress classes I've ever taken at UCLA since Dr. Park prepared us exceptionally well for his midterms. He NEVER tried to trick us on tests, but instead asked questions that directly related to material he covered. His method of teaching was very successful in helping us students

Dr. Park is brilliant. There was never a moment where he didn't have an answer, or a clever way to convey a complex concept to someone who doesn't understand. He's funny, he has great demonstrations, he's always prepared, and he is always willing to answer questions

I have learned a lot and had a great time in his class. Although I am not pursuing a career in physics, this class sparks my interest in learning more about our universe and gravity. It's truly my pleasure to be in Dr Park's Physics 10 class.

Dr. Park was extremely knowledgeable and entertaining. He kept the class engaged while making sure we learned all of the core physics concepts. I will Dr. Park was teaching more courses at extension because I would take them. Overall, Dr. Park is an excellent professor and I would recommend this course to everyone interested in taking a physics

Dr. Park is a very entertaining professor, in a sense that he keeps his students engaged with the topic at hand. I have enjoyed my time with Dr. Park,

Loved the class! Made it super enjoyable to come in on my summer and take this class. He was a pleasure. I didn't like physics before and now with his class he made it super fun and enjoyable. Loved it! Wish he taught all my physics classes.

Dr. Park is probably one of my favorite professors. I wish we had more like him. He makes learning very fun. He has a great sense of humor, energy and wonderful teaching style. I learned a lot of interesting concepts and ideas in our class.

I thought Dr. Park was amazing. I learned so much from this class and his tests were very fair. He was very serious and expected us to take this class seriously as well. He was very patient and made sure we fully understood the material. I would definitely recommend him to others/ take another class with him! Probably one of the best Professor

Dr. Park is what you would call "one of a kind". He makes the class so very interesting and makes sure he implements real life examples that make the class better. The class isn't boring or hard, as people would usually say, because of him. He makes it great, and he is a very good professor.

I thought doctor Park did a great job of explaining concepts and giving us real life applications of them. The course has made me think about the basic aspects of my life in greater detail, and that has made it very valuable educationally.
I learned a lot and got a much better appreciation of Physics !!!

Professor Park made this course a smooth ride. I was worried about doing a physics class, but the professor made sure to keep a slow and steady pace of each topic so we could fully master each topic. His check your understanding packet is amazing because it helps be review important topics and feel confident in knowing each topic in the course. I l
Easily the best teacher I've ever had and the best class I've ever taken. His lectures went by quickly and were always interesting. I would take this class again.

A fabulous instructor in my opinion. He makes the course fun, interactive and the little introductory toastronomy has made me want to go into that field of study. He tries his best to answer questions for students and makes himself available as possible for his students. His passion made this course fun to be in.
The strengths of the professor include his humor and enthusiasm in teaching introductory physics to non-science majors. He is both an amazing professor and person. Thank you

I love what I have learned in this class, I only wish there was more time to cover concepts in more depth and learn other topics. Dr. Park is a great teacher who brings his energy and enthusiasm for physics to the classroom, and keeps us on our toes and engaged.

Dr. Park is by far the best professor I have met at UCLA who changed my academic performance. He creates the class atmosphere to be very exciting and interesting to students. He has a powerful communication skill that draws all students' attention and he definitely is tremendously humorous.

I really appreicate the class has such aa knowledgable professor. It surely broaden my interpretation at the whole Universe. Professor Park explained the material brilliantly. He shared his enthusiasm and knowledge of the subject with us very well
Super Course, Great Professor,Very knowledgeable,Great powerpoint, Very through!!!

Dr. Park is by far one of the best professors in terms of keeping students engaged. Physics can be a difficult subject matter but his demonstrations make it very easy to understand. I don't think a 50 minute class has ever gone by as fast as physics 10 has.

Dr. Park was an enthusiastic, compelling, and hilarious instructor! I am so glad I got to take this course with him it was definitely worthwhile and he made it an excellent class! I learned a lot of material in a short time because Dr. Park presented it in a very effective manner.
Dr Park holds class attention extremely well. He is very entertaining and effective. I was highly satisfied with this course!
Wow... yeah, I'm taking his class, and he's a great Professor, just too smart

I really enjoy professor teahcing. He has a lot of energy keep material simple and  also maintain classroom attention thoroughout the class
Fun Professor.Know what he is doing. I really enjoy every meetings.
HE ROCKS !He covered all modern issues in Physics !! very Funny Professor I love his class

Explain difficult concept with easy step by step method. Learn a   lot He also teach astronomy parts that I like most
You should take this course animation, demo, movie clip so so fun I learned a LOT
Best Professor ever!

Great class full of fun

excellent class with lots of demo

Professor Johns' class is not an easy A but is quite rewarding after you are able to understand the material.

independentcampustutoring is my google address if you need help.

reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She makes law seem so easy. Lectures and cases are so interesting. She posts all lectures to the class website at the beginning of the quarter, which lets you do things at your own pace. SUPER clear and makes things so easy to understand. ONE OF MY FAVORITE PROFESSORS EVER. Is always free to meet and gives tons of useful feedback. 10/10
Fun lectures, and super easy midterm with a nice curve, but walked out of the final feeling physically assaulted. I assume the curve was ridiculous because I still did okay in the end
Yikes, young and yet pedantic, arrogant, unapproachable. Dry, boring, uncessarily complicated. And the lady is a bit loopy and koo-koo, if you know what I mean.
Ugh, what a waste of time!

The textbook assigned does contain very dense legal material, however, she clears up any confusion with her lectures. I wouldn't suggest missing lectures or you'll regret it come midterm and finals time. If you're interested in international law, this class is for you.

Leslie Johns is an excellent professor, but her International Law class is incredibly challenging. There is a TON of reading and it is very dense legal reading that is required in order to do well on the 2 exams. The subject matter is interesting & lectures help to understand the reading. Make sure you have plenty of time to set aside to study!

Professor Terraciano is a great guy. Super friendly, understanding, and helpful. reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Terraciano cares about his students and the material he teaches them. He graded us based on 2 essays, a midterm essay, and a final essay. The work is fairly easy if you attend lecture or do all the readings. Great professor and class. Definitely recommend.
Great professor; glad he was willing to teach this very important material.

Professor Terraciano is very passionate about Latin American Studies. Office hours are group dialogue format. Reading-intensive course. Lectures highlight major themes and critical thinking points. Grading scale: 40% 2 essays, 10% participation, 15% midterm, and 35% final.

Extremely helpful when it came to finals, essays, and midterm. For the final and midterm, he provided a study guide that explain each part of the exam and even included essay questions that would be on the exam. He really knows what he is talking about in the class because his research is in the subject matter covered in the class.

He is very clear with what he expects, uses lectures to complement/clarify readings (of which he is the main author)...excellent readings...exams are straight to the heart of the subject matter, usually requires a paper on a related topic...fair in grading exams and papers.  Super friendly and is always available if you need him.

I took various classes with Dr. Terraciano.  His classes are very interesting, he is very clear, super student friendly.  Students do well not because he is easy, but because he has clear expectations and is always willing to help!
He is very helpful if you go to office hours. If you attend AAP tutoring, he'll review what is on the midterm and the final. Good Guy. Big Heart. Loves to see Chicano/Latino students do well.

She needs to go back to teaching kindergarten. I don't know who she is but she should try sitting in on her own classes. I guarantee by the end of her class, she will be sleeping because of how boring it is. Warning to students planning on taking her class: there's no ice cream party.
uninspiring lectures, uninspiring professor
Unclear grading criteria and bad lectures.

The best professor I have ever met. Legit the GOAT.!!!
She is very caring and understanding
Interesting lectures ranging on many topics. Greatly enjoyed the class and hope to be able to take another class soon.

I loved Professor Placek's class.  She is approachable and well-versed in her subject area. Her lectures are interesting and keep your attention easily. She keeps materials current and all of the studies we read were interesting and not too long. Homework assignments were appropriate in size and quizzes were not very difficult.
Caring, compassionate professor, very easy to communicate with fast feedback. I will take any class of hers she is dedicated to your learning. A true value to UCLA.

Dr. Placek knows how to teach difficult concepts in a way that captivates young adults. reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This class is chaotic and you have no idea what the assignment is asking of you. There are probably seven TRILLION instructions and conditions you have to keep in mind per workbook Prompt. You can find 50,000 more on the syllabus if you want to be extra perfectionist. ONE prompt question is a PAGE full of instructions.

Best professor I've had at UCLA, she makes learning so fun! Don't be scared of her quizzes, I watched the lectures + went over the study guides and got an A or B on them all. She gave a generous 7% of EC. Follow the rubric and you can get a perfect score on her papers. She's so cool & accommodating. I would take all my classes with her if I could.

Great Professor who gives you plenty of help for the weekly quizzes. There's a study guide for it every week that has everything covered for the quiz, so even if you don't want to go through the weekly reading then you can at least grind the study guide.

I enjoyed taking this class over the summer sessions. The course content was very interesting which helped with staying engaged in an asynchronous class. There is a quiz every week regarding that weeks lectures/readings. There is also 2 workbooks that are straightforward and relatively easy if you paid attention throughout the course.
This is a very balanced and informational course. I took it asynchronously and you will be able to obtain an A as long as you watch the lectures fully through. The content is also really interesting!
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Wonderful course. Was very interesting and engaging. Clear that the professor loves the topic she teaches. She is very helpful during office hours. If you remotely pay attention to the lectures, she tells you the answers to most of the quiz content during those lectures. She offers a few opportunities for extra credit also ! great class !!

Professor Placek doesn't just teach what is traditional, she digs deep and it will blow your mind!

Class isn't hard but quizzes can be confusing with the wording.  She's understanding but don't take advantage of her kindness.  Don't expect to get an extension on an assignment if you don't show up to class/never make comments or ask questions.

Professor Placek is very nice and her lectures are actually fun and interesting. Usually we're done a little early too, which is nice. Personally, I thought the class was very easy, but that's because I attend lectures and studied for the quizzes for a few hours. Would take her again, and I probably will in the fall.

Prof. Placek is incredibly engaging. The content material provided is exciting and relevant. She has fascinating guest speakers for both courses. She is very accessible via email or office hours. She records the lectures, is accommodating, and does not count attendance. Stay on top of workbooks and review material! The quizzes require studying.

I love Professor Placek. Her lectures and material are super interesting. She is accommodating, caring, and considerate of all students. She wants everyone to succeed. I've already taken both classes but would immediately take her courses again. Quizzes can be challenging. Make sure to review lectures. I would highly recommend!

Lectures were super engaging. Prof. Placek really cares about all her students and creates a welcoming/safe learning environment in her classroom. Only thing I didn't like all too much were the quizzes. I don't know if I was the only one who struggled that much, but I never did well on them despite how much I studied. Workbooks were good though.

She has one of the clearest grading criteria and the course is about probably something that you never expected to be taught in a university classroom. Do take this course if you are interested in such topics and do not if you hate readings.

Professor Placek is one of the most caring teachers. - Cares about our well-being and grades & always provides additional instructions on assignments. -The lectures are to the point -Tests are a little confusing because of the wording, but if you study the lectures & readings, you are good! Caring, respected, and interesting professor!

Good professor and very caring, the TA's kind of graded harsher than I'm used to. Quizzes sometimes were confusing. Attendance wasn't mandatory and online recordings were provided.
Class seems like it will be super easy in week 1, but the grading is really rough.

One of my favorite professors! She chooses really interesting topics and makes lecture fun. To do well on quizzes, you just need to go to lecture and listen/ take notes. She really cares about what she's teaching as well as the well-being of her students. Thank you Prof Placek for a great quarter!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: OUTSTANDING PROFESSOR EVER! She is generous and friendly if you face any hardship in your quizzes or assignments. I appreciate her caring for a whole semester, I definitely would take her courses in the future again!

Dr. Placek is one of the most generous, understanding, and engaging professors I have taken here at UCLA. She was an amazing lecturer, and had quite a few guest speakers to break up the monotony of school work. She is easy to relate to and fun to listen to. I highly recommend attending lecture... it will benefit you for her tests.

Dr. Placek is tremendously understanding and approachable. She cares about her students, gives extensions on assignments w/o taking points off is very accommodating during exams, and lets you reschedule w/o a question. I took soc. 132 w/ her and it was very interesting. her exams take a bit of getting used to but she applies a curve if needed.
Really enjoyed taking this class and learning about the material!

If you can take any class by Prof Placek pls do! They are so interesting and bring in a good amount of guest lecturers to break up the coursework and keep things fresh. I always felt engaged during class and enjoyed speaking to them after as well. The graders were a little harsh but there are several extra credit opportunities.

Professor P, is one of the most caring professors UCLA has to offer. She is very understanding. Be transparent with her if you are facing any obstacles. Be prepared for weekly monitored test. Do the study guides and take GOOD lecture notes and you should do fine on the test. She grades on a curve. Start your workbooks early.

reviews.comment: Socio - 145This course is structure primarily with a study guide review that are useful for the weekly quizzes and for following lecture. Lecture can cause for you to be distracted since the professor might go on tangents but overall good professor.

Be prepared to write a lot for the workbooks though and start early.

Dr. Placek provides an incredibly supportive classroom environment, which was especially conducive to my learning for this Sociology of Sexuality class. Professor Placek is very caring and accessible. The course is well structured with very interesting topics. There are guest speakers, and the lectures are very inspiring. Would definitely take another course from Professor Placek again.

She gets the struggle of being a student & provides all materials for free. Its clear she's rlly seen it all, nothing phases her &she rlly likes to open up the lecture into class discussion.. Dr. Placek was extremely understanding & extended deadlines when necessary. We Love Dr. Placek !10/10

very good professor

He's very hands on and what he teaches is what we have on the tests. Very easy to talk to and get help from. Over all a really fun class.

Overall I had a good time in his class. The only homework we were ever assigned was readings and 3 essays. He make philosophy fun and engaging. He never brought his own opinion into the lecture. His lectures expressed both sides equally and fair without bias. You can really see the passion in his eyes when he teaches. 10/10!!!!
Have no idea what he was talking about

Good class overall.

Luca Struble gets easily distracted, and sometimes doesn't explain things in a straightforward or clear cut way, but if you ask for help or clarification, then he will always find a way to restate the ideas. Very passionate about Logic and Philosophy.

Used an outdated program as the primary class material. Easily flustered, lectures change direction rapidly and can be very confusing, will answer questions in a way that can make the entire subject more confusing.
Luca is very hard to follow, all over the place, and disorganized. He often went wait way too quickly through material and was very unclear. SAVE YOURSELF. do not take him if you can avoid it.
Solid teacher.

Was really kind throughout the course. You could tell how much he cares about his students succeeding. I know online learning isn't for everyone, but this was definitely one of the best online classes I've taken. He has you apply the concepts to real world scenarios in a relatively low-stakes environment that allows you to learn from your errors.

He never responded to a single request for help, neither through class e-mail, nor work e-mail. Nothing more than a proctor, enjoying benefits of online instruction compensation with close to zero effort. This instructor is not instrumental in providing differentiation for the institution and you are just as likely to succeed anywhere else.
Great on-line class experience if you need to get a stats prerequisite out of the way!

But, the LS30 series in general are some of the hardest classes that I have ever experienced in my whole life. Pay attention and try your absolute hardest because this series will test your sanity.

Siskaki is such a clear and concise lecturer, the material is pretty easy to follow if you've taken calculus before. Paying attention to lectures/not skipping them is crucial for your understanding of the course material. I honestly could've put more effort into studying, but even with the minimal effort, you can FOR SURE get an A in the class.

I actually really enjoyed taking this professor's class last quarter. She is very knowledgeable on the content of this course, despite it being her first time teaching it. She allows time for questions, and gives direct feedback when requested. The effort you put into this course, equates what you will receive from it.

It was easy to pay attention to her lectures, but I also felt like she didn't teach the same content as the other professor did, or at least to the same extent. We at least got points for trying, which lowkey carried my grade.
The professor is very harsh and doesn't explain the material at all. She expects us to ace finals without knowing the subjects.

If there were words to describe Teo! He likes you to show up to class but I wouldn't necessarily call it mandatory, but it gets to the point where you WANT to show up to class. He really is a storyteller and sucks you in in lecture to the point where you don't realize you learned anything until you write a paper. Incredible teacher and human being!

In short, their is nothing NOT to LOVE about Teo.  A great professor, probably the best UCLA offers.  Fun, engaging, incredibly knowledgeable.  Teo is what I will miss most when my days at UCLA are done.  HIGHLY RECOMMENDED!

Dr. Ruiz has been in National Geographic television for the show "TABOO" and was a Princeton University professor in his earlier years. His lectures are so moving, he will almost bring tears to your eyes. He incorporates history and life as a human being into great depth, touching the heart of a student who truly loves knowledge and learning.
COOL PROFESSOR. SUPER FUNNY! KNOWS HOW TO GET HIS STUDENTS' ATTENTION WHILE TEACHING EFFECTIVELY.
Love him, you'll really miss out if you don't take his class. One of the best and most caring professors I've met!
Awesome prof- best ever.
amazing guy :)

He works really hard at appearing to be friendly, but he doesn't teach history.  Rather, history is a platform for him to preach his message of hedonism and revolution.  That's all he cares about and you'd better be able to write about it on his tests if you want a good grade.
No Comments
Best history professor/history chair ever!
Awesome teacher, do your self a favor and sign up for his class.
teo is the greatest! do urself some good and take a class with teo. went to paris with him this past summer...a truly amazing experience!
One of the best teachers ever!
Best. Teacher. Ever.
Don't graduate from UCLA without taking a class with Teo... your tuition is a waste without it.
Teo is the man.
Amazing is the only word for Teo
I love this man...
I love this man...

ive taken most of teo's classes. his 121 series, his religion & magic class, etc. and i even did a research project for him. the one thing about teo that stands out above his colleagues is the amount of caring he has for his students, their well being, and their educational experience.
Teo is so much fun. He is an awesome professor.
What a remarkable, interesting, caring professor.  His lectures are like being transported to another time.  Best professor in my years at UCLA.
My favorite professor at UCLA. Very passionate about teaching and an expert in medieval history.
He is a great professor and advisor.
WOW!! absolutely the teach I had at UCLA.
No Comments
SIMPLY THE BEST TEACHER AT UCLA!
Absolutely the best professor! I love him. He is just great. He is so passionate about the material that there is no way this is boring. He also relates the importance of history now.

Very disappointing experience. I enrolled late due to waitlists and was met with immediate frustration from the professor, who said she was unhappy and suggested I shouldn't have joined. Despite explaining my situation and having an IEP, I felt dismissed and unsupported. The class environment felt tense and unwelcoming, I was belittled and unwelcom
Great lecturer. And she's very helpful during office hours, but beware...if you are talking nonsense she'll call you out. She also teaches at the Law school.

Professor Shiffrin is one of the most intelligent people I have ever met. While this sometimes makes her intimidating, she truly cares about her students and makes every effort to make sure they're understanding the material and are engaged. Excellent professor.

Shiffrin can be cold.  However, I've also experienced how helpful she can be.  I don't know what I did or didn't do to receive the help I did from her, but she's actually quite compassionate.  If you want to be challenged, take her classes.  I took normative ethics and Rawls.  She is definitely something else.

terrible disposition. She is condescending & rude to students,outside and inside class. However, her lectures are crystal clear and the subject material is fascinating. It's a crime her personality isn't as remarkable as her pedagogy.  She is quite brilliant. Genius, in fact. TERRIBLE PROFESSOR AS A WHOLE!! TERRIBLE!!
great teacher
Hes absolutely awful. He talks really fast and expects you to comprehend everything
Lightest class of my life, would 100% take again for the free 100, Terrance Tao ngl the GOAT.
The lectures are pretty difficult but interesting
Overall not that great.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Best professor i have ever met.
Amazing Prof, was an absolute pleasure to work with.
I love this guy.
Just feels like he's spilling his knowledge out rather than teaching. You can be a genius and all, but that doesn't automatically make you a great teacher.
Great Prof.
AMAZING
this guy is the best high school teacher of the past 4 centuries.....i kid you not
It's just plain wonderful to be taught by the smartest, most amicable mathematician alive! Truly amazing.
Amazing professor, this guy is literally a genius. It's amazing to learn from him.
Great
No Comments
He is sweet, personally I found the exams pretty challenging though. They are open note so that is a plus.
Honestly, if you ARENT taking Sherrer, I don't know what you are doing. Dude literally lets you use hand-written notes on tests and curves his exams. PS he is also rlly good looking

Dr. Sherrer is honestly a great professor who understands students have a lot on their plate. The class consisted of two homework assignments, a midterm and a final which are open notes. Attendance isn't mandatory and he is super helpful and posts recordings and slides of all of his lectures to reference. His class was an easy A and he's great.

I hated this teacher, he was not only boring but he took very simple ideas and dragged it out to the point where I was confused and didnt even know what we were taking about. He also can seem to humiliate his students. He also took roll 5 minutes before class started and for all of us coming from other classes were late though his watch is fast
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Sitting through his boring lectures are beyond painful.

As a high school summer studies student I really enjoyed the class. I found Prof. Urdank's lectures interesting. Although depending upon which midterm and final essays were chosen, there was no need to buy all the texts and this should have been made clearer at the start of the quarter.
had this guy in late '80's-early 90's and he's actually quite approachable and helpful and he made me realize that history is a rigorous discipline.

Prof. Urdank took a jockish, boorish, apathetic student and inspired a love of History.  That was me back in the late-80s. He's really laid back, but his methods are Socratic, so be prepared when you get into class or be prepared to shrink down into your chair.  My favorite prof. at UCLA, and still going strong

One of the best and nicest professors at UCLA.  There's a midterm, a short(5-7 pages)"thought piece" paper, and a final.  All the questions are straightforward; there are no tricks.  Lectures are very informative and interesting and the books he assigns very interesting.  Highly recommended.

It is important to address the issues that are affecting our community today. Our parents and grandparents worked hard for us so we can enjoy the privileges we have today in the academic world. 
Some profs. were given a chance under the Diversity, Equity and Inclusion program. Charlene was one of those bc she is a Chicano female of indigent roots.

She really cares about the chicano community.  We are sure that she will take a strong leadership role in  addressing the challenges in our city. Our parents and grandparents have worked a lot for their children and grandchildren and deserve the support of those who by pure luck are doing better and making a lot of money.  Please talk to her asap.

When we come back in the fall, Charlene will discuss the  issues affecting the Los Angeles community these days. She is the leader of The New Aztlan and chair of Chicano Studies. She is giving a voice to the oppressed at UCLA
 Please support her brave endeavors

I agree with all the students who find inspiration on Charlene's projects. I will also apply for the Woodrow Wilson scholarship, because the President's legacy is the foundation of the Chicano/a movement and the privileges we have today. Like Charlene, many of us come from the lower-working class.

I agree with the opinions of fellow students. She is one of the best teachers in the department.
It is a role model for many students who come from disadvantaged backgrounds. The thing to do these days is to apply for scholarships with major foundations like Rockefeller, Ford or Wilson. Those are the best avenues to undo the oppressive thoughts.

Liked the course very much, though it is a lot of work. Charlene's projects are motivating. She is involved in the history of art and the interpretation of decolonizing the minds of the Chicana /o people. Please follow her lead and apply for the Woodrow Wilson Foundation scholarship. It is the accurate path to decolonization of the oppressed

She is excellent in her field. Would take another class with her cz' she is a model for Chicana / Chicano students who are entering college these days. Charlene is admirable, particularly if you check her humble origins. An inspiration for all of us.
Lectures are always packed with info, do not miss!!! She is very nice and very approachable outside of class. By far one of the best professors you will ever take.

Bascara's writing prompts are REALLY confusing. The papers are all pretty long (6-7 pages) and the prompts can be a full page long without clearly stating what he wants or asking a direct question. He also sprung a last minute paper on us in wk 10 with an unclear prompt. The readings are great & hopefully your TA can decode the prompts in section.

Prof. Bascara is a real gem. He keeps class interesting with a variety of teaching strategies, films, and active-learning activities. He inspires us to think critically about the world and make the change that we want to see. He is accessible outside of class and gives feedback that is both helpful and motivational. Hands down the best!
Prof. Bascara is hot!! But yeah, a good teacher also.. take him. you'll enjoy him.

Although Prof. Bascara has a very different style of teaching, he is a fantastic professor nonetheless.  There is a lot of reading but also lots of group assignments and stuff.  Papers are definitely doable and actually interesting.  I would totally recommend him and I'm not an Asian Studies major.  He's incredibly nice/helpful! Take him!!!
good stuff

Brilliant Professor with deep knowledge 
Easy to understand and learn

Professor Lal's lectures are engaging, weaving in his opinions (w/o leaving out other POVs), anecdotes and interesting insights into how the material is relevant today. History, as he says, is the study of the future. You'll study history for ideas, not rote facts. The readings may be a bit heavy esp. if history is not your favorite subject.
He's pretty cool. He's a genius.
Not good for beginners need lot of extra reading,but course highly rearding

His lectures are very fast. He doesn't allow time for questions. His grader is ridiculously unfair. Lal claims there's a 10% portion for attendance, which he doesn't take, yet I never missed a class and my final grade never changed. Be prepared to read several novels and an excruciating amount of dense material. It's an unrealistic load of reading.
amazing guy . must watch his lectures

Very interesting and informative. He recognizes his own bias, and attempts to give both sides. Discussions are mandatory and TAs check to make sure you go to lecture. You should go to lecture because that is the main source of information. Midterms alright, final is difficult but doable. Skim the readings, but GO TO LECTURE!
lectures are very interesting, his opinions and annecdotes are fun to listen to, but work load is heavy
great teacher!!! the best

Profesor LAL is one of the best History instructors ever. He is an intellectual and challenges you like mad balls. He expects his undergrads to work like graduate students. If you are a lazy loafer, don't take any of his classes. However, if your up for a

Lal is a passionate man who really captivates you with the ancectdotes and stories he tells.  He's very approachable and interesting to talk to in and out of class.  Very supportive of student learning.
really good professor
Incredible teacher. Take this class. So willing to help. exams are not bad.

Best class I have ever taken in USC. He was so enthusiastic about what he teaches, always willing to help his students or to discuss anything with his students. Exams are writing essays based on readings and lectures. there's no right/wrong answer but you need to develop your arguments logically. Pay attention to his lectures and do the readings!

Professor Von Blum is one exciting, inspiring and intelligent man. He is extremely engaging with his students, managing to get impressive participation in a class get participation in a 120 person class. He encourages you to think about apathy, inequality, racism, war, patriarchal society. Yells at first, but honestly cares about his students!

Took his course at USC on censorship and the 1st amendment. Would definitely recommend him to any student. He is a very passionate educator with lots of life and educational experience. He talks loud and is bold. Midterm and final only -- essay style. Loved this class.

Professor Von Blum is by far my favorite professor at UCLA. His stories are great, he challenges you with intriguing scenarios, is extremely passionate about his subject, and truly cares about his students. I really have no negatives to say; I am even pursuing an individual studies course with him in the fall!

I took courses with Paul Von Blum at U.C. Berkeley over 35 years ago.  He was a highlight of my college experience.  I still think about the books we read (The Plague, Miss Lonelyhearts, The Loneliness of the Long Distance Runner, etc.) as well as the artists we studied (Hogarth, Goya, Daumier, etc.)  Thank you, Paul, for everything!

I took several of his pol sci classes at UC Irvine 20 yrs ago and thought so highly of him that I recently looked him up to see if he was still at UCLA.  He challenged us with moral and ethical scenarios which made us think and some didn't like it.  I loved it and thought he was very brave to push the envelope on taboo subjects.BEST Teacher EVER!

I took Paul's class over 13 years ago.  He helped shape my understanding of the world.  I learned a lot about Paul Robeson, who continues to inspire me, and about public art.  Good teachers question the way we think about the world and they force us to ask ourselves questions far after the exams are over.  I recommend that everyone take him.

Anyone reading this....I took two courses with Paul in the mid-70's at Berkeley.  35 years later, I can tell you he changed my life.  What he teaches is how to think and communicate.  Whatever you do in your life...those are the most important tools.

Itrs professors like Von Blum that make UCLA prestigious and worthy of anything. He's been in the UC system I believe for over 20 years begining with Berkeley.. part of the Civil Rights movement.. I cant say more than passionate & experienced to describe him. You must take a class with him to make your education worth anything!

At first I did not want to go to class during the summer because basically I did not have to for the grade was based on two essays. But the passion and quality of this teacher made me want to go just to hear him talk and engage in the interesting conversations. I RECOMMEND THIS TEACHER!

This class was soo long that it could get kinda boring, but professor Von Blum is the type that will go out of his way to help you understand and grasp the information. I recomend him just another course.
EXCELLENT PROF! MUST TAKE AT LEAST ONCE WHILE AT UCLA!

I would not call this man a teacher, but more a speaker. He is a very passionate speaker. However, I had no idea what he wanted us to retain. I did not know what to take down in my notes. I think it is completely unfair that some TAs grade harsher than others. It is an easy grade if you have easy TAs. If you are a great writer, you should be fine.

He's an amazing teacher! The discussions in class - the way he leads them w/out you even noticing it -, his points and questions, the topics, the works, etc. were excellent. AND he's  there for his students anytime.

I disagree with the previous review. My discussion sections with my TA helped glue everything from lecture and readings together. I did very well in the class and found my TA to be very accessible and helpful. If you write well and convincingly, participate in discussion, and attend sections, you'll get an easy A.

He is very passionate about his teaching, but the grades are not in line with what you learn.  Poor TAs that lack communication skills.  He is a great guy with interesting lectures, but the graded system is all subjective and is not objective.
No Comments
EASY B HARD A
very captive speaker with lots of interesting stories.  class is just two papers and a quiz, class can inspire you.  is always open to help students after class
he's one of the best professor here.

Von Blum is by far one of the best professors at UCLA.  he give his lectures with much energy and her sincerely cares about his students.  he has a passion for what he does and what he teaches- and it shows.  if you haven't taken a class with von blum, you've missed out.
After graduating from UCLA, Von Blums classes are probably the fondest *coursework* memory that I have.  He really enhanced my college experience.

Who takes you on a field trip to LA county Jail for a Speech class - Prof. Von Blum. This man poseses a gift for teaching. He inspires and excites his students through the greatest lessons of all, that which come from real life experience. I'll never forget some of the discussions we had in and out of class. How many teachers truly make that kind of an impact? I hope he'd take pride knowing he's partialy to blame for the passion (and stories) I bring into my own classroom.
Amazing professor. Amazing man. Like no professor you've ever had.

One-of-a-kind professor-- Exemplary man. The most profound and influential teacher i've had.  I'm now a professor in San Diego and I continue to reference Von Blum as an inspiration. Unfortunately, the UCLA administration fails to recognize that it's the people like Paul who make the university honorable, respected and prestigious.

Thrilled to see the students at UCLA recognize quality - had Von Blum over ten years ago at UCI and we all worshipped him.  I am now a professor on the East Coast and he is still the best I've ever seen.  He is a living legend, so make your time with him count.  He is a rare gift.

I love this professor, his classes are interesting, and he brings together a wide range of knowledge and integrates them into his classes. and he teaches everything! I especically liked his women's studies and art history courses, and I guess the comm classes were awesome tooo. You rock Paul!
well he is my dad so I guess you can say that he's also my professor. If any of you students have heard any stories about me in your class, remember that it's just a story.  love you dad
this professor is excellent, and unlike another reviewer's opinion, I like his personal stories because he is actually interesting
anybody like hearing stories?  because that's what his lectures are.  very opinionated man, prefers to share past experiences rather than teach anything.
we loev you BLUUUUUUUM!!

This professor truly cares about his students - like no other I have met at UCLA or anywhere. He even spend his off hours helping students, taking students on field trips (four field trips in one quarter!) The best teacher I've had!
No Comments
Professor Von Blum is quite refreshing and he doesn't believe in final exams.

Brian a smart guy, and his lectures and written homeworks force you to learn a lot. Unfortunately, the written homeworks are a lot of work, and they generally don't match what you do on the quizzes. He also doesn't give clear feedback on quizzes, and the class felt disorganized. Our grades were not posted online, and deadlines were always changing.

Professor Van Koten is a young, new professor. He is clear with his examples and he's always willing to answer questions. His exams, however, are very difficult. He's more focused on proofs . His class is kind of demanding since he has quizzes every week. It was the first time I struggled in a math class. I worked very hard and ended up with a B.

He teaches well and has good examples. The problem is his tests. The av. on the midterm/final was about 50%. I got a C+ which is super disappointing because I've never gotten below an A in math ever before. His tests are SUPER hypothetical, confusing, and downright almost impossible to pass. May the curve be ever in your favor.
Van Koten is a new professor, and he is very clear with his examples. He focuses on theory and proofs, rather than routine calculation. If you do the homework in his class, you will be fine
Hes a very good professor! Amazing class! I feel like I am learning more than being stressed about silly exams.

His accent is fine, the subject is demanding so don't no slackers need apply. The more I read up in this subject, the more I find his name cross referenced everywhere! He's an expert for sure and can answer any question your throw at him provided it's relevant. Difficult, but you'll learn so much. That's why were here after all right?

He is extremely well-educated and provides ample background on everything he teaches, going beyond dry facts. His attention to detail and knowledge of entertaining tidbits of history make for a very stimulating class. You write a lot but he is not a hard grader at all, and very encouraging. He is enthusiastic + informed. He made me a better writer.
Obviously LOVES what he teaches!
Very knowledgeable and helpful.
Personally found the subjects provided mind numbing.  Maybe it is his heavy accent, but I didn't feel like he engaged the class to participate in conversation/discussion of presented material

Some students felt that the thick German accent and esoteric nature of his teaching were difficult to understand.  But he was one of my favorite teachers, who got beyond the boring art-historical information and went into symbolism.

He gives 6 long tough assignments instead of a final but you're allowed to work with others and get help during office hours. If you like the material and are comfortable with proofs then it's gonna be a great time cause Liam is very enthusiastic about the topic and it shows.

Very chill teaching style and super reasonable guy. Frequently checked in with the class to make sure things were going well and that he wasn't going too fast or too slow. We had 6 assignments that made up our entire grade which was a welcomed change of pace for me. He gives lots of help on the assignments if you ask for it.
Professor Watson is at UBC Math dept now and is an excellent professor. Great humour, very polite, quick to get back to you. Just all around excellent asset for UBC.
Professor Watson is really talented at presenting lectures. And his exams are nice and closely related to the course materials. I had a great time having his class.

I don't know what the other comment is talking about I don't think i've ever seen professor yoon be anything other than friendly. The class was a good pace and the lectures were always interesting because she's good at adding personal anecdotes here and there. The only thing is the tests are a bit hard but straightforward if you study everything.
Mrs Yoon is nice but sometime very strict and cranky or moody. This class was all about memorization. If you are a native Korean speaker you will do fine. Overall it was fun.
Very helpful.Willing to listen and work with you.Highly recommend.
i hate her she is awful
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Yan lao shi is amazing! She is so caring and helpful. Her lectures are all in Mandarin and she pushes you to speak as much Chinese as possible, so you have to study a lot in order to keep up. Exams are difficult but not impossible, and quizzes are the hardest part of the class, but she drops your two lowest quiz scores. Amazing teacher overall!
Shen laoshi is an inspiration. She cares about her students more than any teacher I've ever met and is so wholesome and kind. I learned so much and never felt that I wouldn't be able to suceed.

Lovely and caring lady but very intense Chinese course. She teaches the class almost entirely in Mandarin and covers a ton of material. It's very fast-paced... You will learn a lot but will fall easily behind if you don't keep up. The exams are brutal. Make sure your schedule is flexible and dedicate much time to studying otherwise you will burn.
I know that Prof Goyal yells at department staff, showing elitism and poor character.

She hate females. Incredibly boring and opinionated. Many of us met after class for "therapy" to decompress because she is an awful person. Thank God I will never have to take her again, could not pay me enough. Her criticism is not helpful or contractive. Does not lend itself to improving. Take only if you are a masochist.

This class was taken on Zoom due to the coronavirus. Professor Goyal was very understanding and nice considering everyone was quarantined during class. We read a lot of books and had mostly lectures/discussions each class. If you do the work on time and put in effort, you'll get an A. She is not a hard grader. I recommend her
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Goyal is extremely knowledgeable about African American History. Always available during office hours, she genuinely cares about students. She is a fair grader and gives great feedbacks. She is brilliant and her passion for the topic is contagious.I loved her class and style of teaching.

WONDERFUL! Clear, organized, prepared, she makes class a breeze. The books were great and her lectures were heavenly. Study guides match the tests so easy As. Great to talk to in office hours, great to do honors with, GREAT PERSON. She was concerned for our learning and the whole class felt appreciated and respected by her.
She is a great, extremely intelligent teacher.  However, it is essential one keeps decorum and writes well in order to succeed in her class.

The readings were very interesting. That's about it. The seminars were super boring- half of the class would fall asleep. I found more amusement watching people's heads bobbing then I did trying to decipher what she was saying.

Yirush is great! Super helpful in office hours. His enthusiasm for early American history is infectious. Get ready to read a LOT every week, 150 pages a week of dense primary source material is not unusual. But if you enjoy American history you will love his class, one of my favorite profs at UCLA
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: You will do a lot of work (if you want an A) and learn alot. Great professor!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Yirush is very challenging, but he makes you a better writer while teaching history in an organized, logical, but interesting and fun way. His use of primary sources is incredibly beneficial, and he essentially provides you with all the evidence you need for your papers. Again, he is tough, but his criticism of your writing is truly a gift.

One of the toughest classes I've ever had, but it was worth it because it made me a better writer.  It also made me better at analyzing primary sources.  He pushes you to give him your very best, although if you're looking to help your GPA more than your writing skills take someone else.

I enjoyed this class alot. Professor Yirush really knows the subject and is passionate about it. There is a lot of class discussion and he likes to call on the unsuspecting (always be prepared). Lots of memorizing dates and reading from primary sources. I would definitely take his class again.
Yirush is an excellent lecturer - kept things interesting and fast paced.  He is tough but fair & consistent.  I would highly recommend this Professor.

He was very excited about the material, which makes the class more interesting. Make sure to do all the readings. He gives a helpful study guide a few days before the final so you can narrow down your studies. Very friendly guy.
Really knows the subject but could  lighten up on the primary source readings. He seems to take this a little too seriously. Attendance is mandatory.
